dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can.
instructions.note: Solution = the folder this file sits in. Table / object name = this file's name.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: A dependency between two pieces of work — one blocks the other, or the two merely relate. When the two items sit in different Solutions this is a cross-repository dependency, which is derived from their Solutions rather than stored here.

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data.

entries.rules: must reference a WorkItem row; must not equal toWorkItemId
entries.description: The work item the dependency belongs to — the one that waits, for a 'blocks' link
'blocks' means the target cannot start until the source is done, so it orders work and must stay acyclic. 'relatesTo' is worth knowing about but implies no order, so loops of it are harmless

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Until the link is removed or either work item is deleted. Deleting a work item removes every link touching it — a link to a deleted item is not a dependency, it is a dangling row.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields that need extra protection?

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.answer: Both work items exist and are different. The same pair may hold at most one link of each kind. 'blocks' links must never form a cycle, directly or transitively — a ring of blocking work is a plan where nothing can start. 'relatesTo' carries no such rule because it implies no order. Whether a link crosses repositories is worked out from the two items' Solutions and never stored, so it cannot fall out of step.

changesOverTime.guidance: Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change.

rounds.change: New: Planning becomes execution-only and needs to show work waiting on other work, including work landing in a different repository.
